questions.q: Whose mother is Kaushalya in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya is the mother of Rama, the eldest son of King Dasharatha.

questions.q: Who was Kaushalya's husband?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya was a queen and the wife of King Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

questions.q: What was Kaushalya's status among the queens of Dasharatha?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya was the eldest and senior-most queen of King Dasharatha.

questions.q: In which kingdom did Kaushalya live as queen?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya was a queen of Ayodhya, the capital of the Kosala kingdom.

questions.q: How is Kaushalya generally described in temperament?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya is portrayed as a gentle and devout queen of great piety.

questions.q: Which of Dasharatha's sons did Kaushalya give birth to?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya was the mother of Rama, while the other sons were born to Kaikeyi and Sumitra.

questions.q: How many years of exile did Rama have to undergo, causing Kaushalya great sorrow?
questions.explanation: Rama was exiled to the forest for fourteen years, which deeply grieved Kaushalya.

questions.q: Which co-wife of Kaushalya demanded that Rama be exiled?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi, another of Dasharatha's queens, demanded Rama's exile to install her own son Bharata.

questions.q: What did Kaushalya wish to see Rama become before his exile was ordered?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya rejoiced that Rama was to be crowned king, before Kaikeyi's demand reversed the plan.

questions.q: Who was the other gentle queen of Dasharatha, often Kaushalya's close companion?
questions.explanation: Sumitra, mother of Lakshmana and Shatrughna, was a kindly co-queen close to Kaushalya.

questions.q: Which divine offering enabled Kaushalya and the other queens to bear sons?
options: Sacred payasam from a fire sacrifice
A boon from a forest sage's curse
A golden fruit from the sea
questions.explanation: The Putrakameshti yajna produced a divine payasam (kheer) that Dasharatha shared among his queens.

questions.q: What portion of the sacred payasam did Kaushalya receive, leading to Rama's birth?
questions.explanation: In the common tradition Kaushalya, as senior queen, received half of the divine payasam, and her son Rama was the foremost portion of Vishnu.

questions.q: On the morning of Rama's intended coronation, what was Kaushalya doing when she heard of the exile?
options: Performing worship and prayers for Rama's welfare
questions.explanation: Kaushalya was engaged in devout worship for Rama's prosperity when the calamitous news reached her.

questions.q: When Rama came to bid farewell, what did the grieving Kaushalya initially urge him to consider?
options: Remaining with her rather than going to the forest
questions.explanation: Overcome with grief, Kaushalya pleaded that Rama stay with her, but he gently upheld his duty to obey his father.

questions.q: What blessing did Kaushalya finally give Rama as he departed for exile?
options: Her blessings that dharma would protect him
A demand that he never return
questions.explanation: Mastering her sorrow, Kaushalya blessed Rama and prayed that the dharma he upheld would guard him in the forest.

questions.q: Who accompanied Rama into exile along with him, easing some of Kaushalya's worry?
questions.explanation: Rama was accompanied into the forest by his wife Sita and his devoted brother Lakshmana.

questions.q: What happened to Dasharatha soon after Rama left for the forest?
options: He led an army to retrieve Rama
He abdicated and became a hermit
He banished Kaikeyi from the kingdom
questions.explanation: Unable to bear separation from Rama, King Dasharatha died of grief shortly after the exile began.

questions.q: Which earlier deed of Dasharatha did Kaushalya hear connected to the curse that he would die in grief over a son?
options: The slaying of the demon Tataka
The accidental killing of Shravana Kumara
The burning of the Khandava forest
questions.explanation: Dasharatha's accidental killing of the youth Shravana Kumara brought the blind parents' curse that he too would die grieving for a son.

questions.q: How did Kaushalya treat Sita, Rama's wife, in the palace?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya regarded Sita with warm, motherly affection and grieved that she too must endure the forest.

questions.q: When Bharata returned and learned of the exile, how did Kaushalya at first respond to him?
options: With initial reproachful grief that softened to forgiveness
questions.explanation: Kaushalya at first reproached the grieving Bharata, but seeing his blamelessness and sorrow she forgave and consoled him.

questions.q: Of which great cosmic deity is Rama, Kaushalya's son, regarded as an incarnation?
questions.explanation: Rama, the son of Kaushalya, is revered as an avatara (incarnation) of Lord Vishnu.

questions.q: During the long exile, how is Kaushalya generally described as enduring her sorrow?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya bore the separation from Rama with dignified sorrow, sustained by her devotion and faith.

questions.q: By which name, derived from her ancestry or kingdom, is Kaushalya often known, reflecting her royal origin?
options: Princess associated with the Kosala line
questions.explanation: Kaushalya's name is traditionally associated with Kosala lineage, and she is honored as a princess of royal birth.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which ritual horse sacrifice did Dasharatha first perform in his attempt to obtain sons before the Putrakameshti?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha first performed the Ashvamedha (horse sacrifice), followed by the Putrakameshti that granted the sons born to Kaushalya and the other queens.

questions.q: Which sage advised and conducted the son-granting sacrifice that led to Rama's birth from Kaushalya?
questions.explanation: The sage Rishyasringa was invited to conduct the Putrakameshti sacrifice that resulted in the birth of Rama and his brothers.

questions.q: After Dasharatha's death, where did Kaushalya remain while Bharata governed as regent in Rama's name?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya remained in Ayodhya during the exile years while Bharata ruled as regent, placing Rama's sandals on the throne.

questions.q: In Valmiki's account, what did Kaushalya famously do to seek divine protection for Rama before he set out for exile?
options: She performed worship and tied protective rites invoking the gods and dharma upon him
She gave him a sword forged by Vishvakarma
She sent soldiers to guard him secretly
questions.explanation: Kaushalya performed sacred rites and invoked the protection of the deities, the directions, and dharma itself upon Rama before his departure.

questions.q: Which co-queen's son ultimately refused the throne out of devotion, vindicating Kaushalya's faith in family dharma?
questions.explanation: Bharata, Kaikeyi's son, refused to usurp the throne and ruled only as Rama's regent, honoring the dharma Kaushalya cherished.

questions.q: When Rama at last returned to Ayodhya after fourteen years, what was the nature of Kaushalya's reunion with him?
options: A joyful reunion crowned by his coronation
A bitter quarrel over the throne
questions.explanation: Kaushalya joyfully welcomed Rama home after his exile, and her long sorrow ended with his coronation as king of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Which emblem and qualities are traditionally associated with Kaushalya as the gentle senior queen and mother of the divine Rama?
options: The lotus, symbol of purity and devotion
questions.explanation: Kaushalya is associated with the lotus, an emblem of purity and devotion befitting her gentle, pious character.
